serving.description: 1 set meal (Gyudon bowl + 2 half-boiled eggs + miso soup or drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal serving of approximately 650 g, comprising a Gyudon rice bowl topped with simmered beef and onions, two half-boiled eggs, and a miso soup or beverage.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — SR Legacy and Foundation Foods composite estimate for beef and rice dish with egg
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry for Gyudon combo exists. Per-100g values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data for cooked short-grain white rice (FDC ID 168878), cooked beef (FDC ID 174032), whole egg (FDC ID 748967), and miso soup (FDC ID 172459), weighted to reflect the approximate composition of a standard Gyudon set meal (rice ~45%, beef+onion sauce ~35%, eggs ~15%, miso soup ~5%). Input values from the source record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-checked against this USDA composite and retained where consistent. Sodium value of 276.9 mg/100g is consistent with soy-sauce-based Japanese dishes per USDA reference data.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry for Gyudon combo. GI of 72 is assigned based on the dominant carbohydrate component: Japanese short-grain white rice, which has a published GI of approximately 72 (University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson et al. 2008, Am J Clin Nutr). The beef, egg, and miso components contribute negligible carbohydrate and do not materially alter the GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (72 × 90.0) / 100 ≈ 65.0, where carbohydrate per 650g serving = 13.85 × 6.5 = 90.0g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 276.9 mg/100g (approximately 1800 mg per 650g serving) from soy sauce, miso paste, and dashi, exceeding 75% of the 2400 mg daily sodium limit in a single meal.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced soy sauce and low-sodium miso, avoid drinking the miso soup broth, and pair the meal with potassium-rich foods such as banana or leafy greens to partially offset the sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Japanese short-grain white rice (GI ~72) contributes approximately 90g carbohydrate per serving with only 4g dietary fibre, producing a very high glycemic load of ~65 per meal.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should request a reduced rice portion or substitute with lower-GI grain, retain the beef and egg components which slow glucose absorption, and avoid mirin-sweetened sauce to limit added sugar intake.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 1.54 g/100g (approximately 10g per serving) from thinly sliced beef and egg yolks, combined with high sodium (~1800 mg per serving), presents a combined cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size, request leaner beef cuts, retain the egg white while reducing yolk consumption, and avoid the miso soup broth to reduce sodium intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Half Boiled Egg Gyudon Combo (半熟卵牛丼コンボ) is a Japanese set meal served in Singapore, consisting of a Gyudon rice bowl — thinly sliced beef and onions simmered in a savoury-sweet sauce of soy sauce, mirin, dashi, and sugar — served over steamed Japanese short-grain white rice, topped with a half-boiled egg. The combo includes an additional half-boiled egg and a miso soup or beverage. The dish delivers a tender, umami-rich beef topping with a silky, runny egg yolk that enriches the sauce when broken. The rice is soft and slightly sticky, characteristic of Japanese short-grain varieties. Per 100g, the dish provides approximately 115 kcal, 5.9g protein, 3.9g fat, and 13.9g carbohydrate, with sodium at 276.9 mg reflecting the soy-sauce and miso base. A full 650g serving delivers approximately 750 kcal, 38g protein, 25g fat, and 90g carbohydrate. The high glycemic load (~65 per serving) and elevated sodium (~1800 mg per serving) make this dish a concern for individuals managing type 2 diabetes or hypertension. The red meat component is a consideration for those with rheumatoid arthritis or cardiovascular disease. Culturally, Gyudon is a staple of Japanese fast-casual dining, popularised by chains such as Yoshinoya and Sukiya, and widely available in Singapore's Japanese restaurant segment.

confidence_reason: No exact approved-source entry for Gyudon combo exists in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database. Per-100g values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data for cooked short-grain rice, cooked beef, whole egg, and miso soup, weighted by approximate dish composition. Input record provenance was ai_generated at confidence 0.7; values are consistent with USDA component estimates and have been retained. GI is analogously estimated from Japanese short-grain white rice (University of Sydney GI Database).
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite Gyudon dish is not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for the assembled dish as of 2024. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium data for this composite dish not available in the approved source consulted. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this composite Gyudon set meal not available in USDA FoodData Central for the assembled dish as of 2024. All core macros (protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) are non-zero and reflect the mixed composition of rice, beef, egg, and miso components. trans_fat_g of 0.08g/100g is a low but non-zero value consistent with naturally occurring trans fats in ruminant beef, per USDA SR Legacy data.
